description_long: This imposing stone castle features a rectangular layout with four large cylindrical corner towers and four additional mid-wall towers, all topped with crenellated battlements. The main entrance is centrally located on one of the longer walls, flanked by two towers and accessed through a fortified gate. The walls are constructed from light-colored stone blocks, giving the structure a robust and historic appearance. Arrow slits are visible along the upper sections of the walls, providing defensive vantage points. The central courtyard is partially visible through the entrance, and the upper levels include small windows, suggesting interior chambers. The overall design is reminiscent of Medieval European fortifications, emphasizing defense and grandeur.
description: A large, rectangular stone castle with eight cylindrical towers, crenellated battlements, and a fortified central entrance. The light stone walls and defensive features evoke a classic Medieval fortress.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance gate, flanked by two large cylindrical towers and the central courtyard visible through the gate.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of the cylindrical towers and the solid stone walls with battlements.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with two towers and the absence of a main entrance, emphasizing the defensive perimeter.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the castle's depth, the arrangement of towers, and the crenellated parapets along the walls.
